evidence: Solventum intends to devote significant resources to develop and deploy cloud, edge and software solutions in its healthcare solutions.
The deployment of AI, machine learning or other emerging technologies into Solventum's products and services will require additional investment and increase its costs.

Solventum has incorporated AI into certain products in the Health Information Systems business segment and certain of its digital offerings and expects to continue to build AI into additional products.

Health Information Systems provides healthcare systems with software solutions including computer-assisted physician documentation, direct-to-bill and coding automation, classification methodologies, speech recognition, and data visualization platforms.
Solventum has incorporated AI into certain products in the Health Information Systems business segment and certain of its digital offerings.
Our solutions integrate digital processes, AI-enabled capabilities and data in multiple ways and across different parts of the healthcare industry.

Any disruption or failure in the AI, machine learning, or other emerging technologies...could adversely impact Solventum's business, including as a result of flawed AI algorithms, insufficient or biased datasets, malfunctions or manipulations, unintentional release of confidential information.
The emergence of new AI-enabled competitors and business model disruption, particularly for our HIS business; changes in the business environment related to disruptive technologies, such as AI.

Solventum is required to comply with increasingly complex and changing legal and regulatory requirements...including for the development and use of AI...the European Union's Artificial Intelligence Act and various other country-specific requirements.
